skill.name: azure-ai-contentsafety-java
skill.description: Build content moderation applications with Azure AI Content Safety SDK for Java
skill.summary: Implement text and image content moderation using Azure AI Content Safety SDK for Java with harm detection and blocklist management.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer produced false positives due to misinterpreting Markdown documentation. All 33 external_commands findings are Java code examples in Markdown fences, not shell execution. Network findings are example URLs for configuration. Environment variable access is standard Azure SDK pattern. No actual security issues detected.

content.user_title: Build content moderation apps with Azure AI
content.value_statement: Moderate harmful content automatically in your applications. Azure AI Content Safety SDK for Java detects hate speech, violence, sexual content, and self-harm with configurable severity thresholds.
actual_capabilities: Analyze text for hate, violence, sexual, and self-harm content categories
Analyze images for harmful visual content with severity scoring
Create and manage custom blocklists for domain-specific terms
Configure output severity levels (0-7 scale for text, 0-6 for images)
Integrate with Azure DefaultAzureCredential for secure authentication
Handle API errors with detailed HTTP response information
limitations: Requires Azure subscription and Content Safety resource provisioning
Blocklist changes take approximately 5 minutes to propagate
API calls incur Azure service costs based on volume
Image analysis requires image data or accessible blob URL

use_cases.title: Social Platform Moderation
use_cases.description: Automatically screen user-generated posts and comments for harmful content before publication. Configure severity thresholds to match community guidelines.

use_cases.title: Customer Support Filtering
use_cases.description: Detect abusive messages in support tickets and chat systems. Route flagged content to human reviewers while allowing legitimate messages through.

use_cases.title: Marketplace Content Review
use_cases.description: Scan product listings, reviews, and images for policy violations. Maintain custom blocklists for brand-specific prohibited terms.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this text for harmful content using Azure AI Content Safety: [paste text]. Return severity scores for each category.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick content check for a single text input

prompt_templates.title: Configured Category Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze [text] for hate and violence categories only. Use 8 severity levels output. Stop if blocklist hit occurs.
prompt_templates.scenario: Targeted analysis with specific categories and blocklist enforcement

prompt_templates.title: Image Moderation from URL
prompt_templates.prompt: Check the image at [URL] for harmful content. Report categories with severity >= 4. Format results as a moderation decision.
prompt_templates.scenario: Remote image analysis with threshold-based filtering

prompt_templates.title: Blocklist Management Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a blocklist named [name] with description [desc]. Add these terms: [terms]. Then analyze [text] against this blocklist with halt on hit enabled.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complete workflow from blocklist creation to enforced analysis

output_examples.input: Analyze user comment for hate speech and violence
output_examples.output: Hate: Severity 2 (low confidence), Violence: Severity 0 (not detected). Content passes moderation threshold.
Sexual category detected at Severity 6. Recommendation: Block content per policy threshold of 4.
Blocklist hit detected: Item ID abc123 matched term variant. Content blocked per haltOnBlocklistHit setting.

best_practices: Read Azure SDK documentation for authentication and client configuration before implementation
Set severity thresholds based on your risk tolerance - severity 4+ is typical for strict moderation
Allow 5 minutes for blocklist changes to propagate before testing new entries
Cache analysis results for repeated content to reduce API calls and latency
anti_patterns: Hardcoding credentials instead of using environment variables or DefaultAzureCredential
Requesting all harm categories when only specific ones are needed - increases latency and cost
Blocking severity 0 content - this creates false positives for borderline cases
Synchronous API calls in high-throughput scenarios - use async patterns for batch processing

faq.question: What Azure subscription do I need for Content Safety?
faq.answer: You need an Azure subscription with a Content Safety resource provisioned. Free tier includes 5,000 transactions per month for testing.

faq.question: How do I authenticate the SDK?
faq.answer: Use either API key authentication with KeyCredential or Azure AD authentication with DefaultAzureCredential for production environments.

faq.question: Can I customize harm detection thresholds?
faq.answer: Yes, set outputType to 8 severity levels and apply your own threshold filtering. The SDK returns numeric severity scores you can evaluate.

faq.question: How quickly do blocklist updates take effect?
faq.answer: Blocklist changes typically propagate within 5 minutes. Plan accordingly when adding newly discovered problematic terms.

faq.question: Does the SDK support async operations?
faq.answer: Yes, the Azure SDK supports asynchronous patterns. Use async client methods for non-blocking operations in high-throughput applications.

faq.question: What image formats are supported?
faq.answer: The API accepts common image formats including PNG, JPEG, and BMP. Images can be provided as binary data or accessible blob URLs.
